## [00:00 - 03:00]

So guys, welcome back to Six Figure SMA. Today we're going to be discussing what would success look like and this is a very, very important question to go over with your clients because look, the thing is most of the time, like look, if I give you $100 right now and then tomorrow I go back and I go, dude, I actually need $50 of those back, you still gained $50. The funny thing is us as humans, we're very loss averse, right? So whatever we're expecting, if we get anything less than that, even though if it's still a substantial gain, we freak the fuck out, right? And that's the exact same thing with your clients. Look, you might be making them a great return on investment, whether that be a social ROI or you know, very clear cut ROI through paid traffic. But if they were expecting more, doesn't matter, right? I've had occasions with clients where there's a 30 X ROI on a campaign. I've had occasions with myself where it's like a 50 X ROI on advertising campaigns online. Right? These things happen, right? ## [03:00 - 06:01]

And yeah, they just need to find a reasonable cost to acquire a customer. And anything below the LTV is technically profitable right now. As I said, you know, I like to go for a 2XRI because one thing about the cost to acquire a customer or one thing about the LTV is it doesn't take into consideration referrals and recommendations. When you bring someone say to a gym, if I like a gym, I'm telling everyone I know. My accountant, love them. I tell all other business, online business owners about my accountant and I refer the company. Right? I refer the accounting firm. Same thing with like, I know me specifically, if I like something and I've actually tested and vetted it, I tell so many people. Right? But that never gets taken into consideration in terms of the lifetime value of me as a customer. Right? So reasonable cost to acquire a customer, pretty much I like to go for just half of what their lifetime value is. And anything below there, as I said, you're pretty much good to go because once again, another thing it doesn't take into consideration is the fact that they're actually going to refer people. Right? So first thing you just need to kind of go over with them is what is a reasonable cost to acquire a customer and that gives you really good indication when you run Facebook ads, you know how much a lead can cost you or stuff like that.
